Title: Were Quests
SubTitle: Lycan, Were-Hunter, and Vampire Quests
Bearwere:
**Quest Name:** Bear Were Quest  
**Level:** 30  
**Class:** ALL  
**Quest NPC:** Keiv Bearclaw  
**Location:** Keiv is in the village south of CLW, North Wilderlands  
**Tail NPC:** Dorandas in the Unkemp Village, Unkempt North (S)

---

Speak with **Keiv Bearclaw** in the village south of CLW (**North Wilderlands (S)**). He is found in the same village where Sissy Harper is for the 30 class quest, just north of Sissy's door, by a well. He will send you north to roam the area around CLW (NE) until you receive a pop-up (run past CLW like you're going towards goblins). Doing so triggers two bears — **Barbiedolls Paw** & **Flurry**, each of whom con the same level as the person who triggers them. The pop-up is on a 30-minute timer. Defeat the bears and return to Keiv.

Keiv will then send you to **Anu Village** to seek out a similar pop-up. Locate the observatory in the north, and right before it is a hill that glows blue with stones on top — roam around the area until you receive a pop-up. You will again be fighting mobs similar to the first pop-up triggered event. Defeat the bears and return to Keiv.

Keiv will now ask you to speak with **Dorandas** in **Unkempt North (S)**. Log the tail quest and begin farming the appropriate bearweres until you receive the infection pop-up. If Dorandas will not speak with you, you'll need to hunt bearweres first to raise your faction with **Wards of the Dawn** to 0 (maybe 150?).

Killing **weres that con YELLOW** to you is key. Yellow-conned weres give you the greatest chance of infection. Check your level against the zone information below and ensure you are hunting Yellow-Con weres, otherwise you will only benefit from faction. If you are already infected, and faction is all you need, it is easier to hunt in a lower-level zone.

---

### On Having a Level 60 in the Group

There was always a rumor that having a higher-level toon in your group to help with the killing would mean your chances of infection would drop almost to nil. Well, here's some good information:

> **Dustyspheres** says: The rumor that having a level 60 in your low-level group affects your chance of infection just isn't true. The last 9 infections I've gotten or helped get were all with toons in one group, invised and on Auto-follow. These toons ranged from 33–39 and were on AF with a 60 bard that did all of the killing. All they did was loot tails and they still got infected. Some in just a few kills, and most before anyone even had the required 1k faction to accept the infection.

---

### "Were" to Get Infected

**Level 30–40**  
- Fort Alliance  
- North Wilderlands  
- Unkempt Glade  
- Unkempt North  

**Level 40–50**  
- Elephant Graveyard  
- Salisearaneen  

**Level 50–60**  
- Stoneburnt Mountains *(lvl 60 - confirmed)*  
- The Warrens *(lvl 51 - confirmed)*  

> *See note below on doing bear at 60.*

---

After you have become infected, you will be asked to defeat **Mirin** in **Unkempt North** (see note below). The mob cons the same level as the person who triggers him, so bring assistance.

**Orphanmaker1** says: Mirin spawns in *Unkempt North (SW)* just a bit east of the bandit village (at night time they are bearweres).

---

### Infection Check

You can also check for infection in **Oasis**. Go to the campfire near the water — several NPCs stand around it.  
- **Advisor Gerahl** checks for *general infection* (any were).  
- **Advisor Wanda** checks specifically for *bear infection*.

---

### Note on Dorandas

If you get the popup that Dorandas has something urgent to tell you:

- If all he'll do is give you the tail quest again, check first — is your faction with **Wards of the Dawn** 1000 or more? If not, get more faction by killing weres.
- If your faction is good, delete the tail quest in your quest log and re-speak to Dorandas, who will now give you the final part of your infection quest.
- When Dorandas gives his speech **do NOT take the antidote**, or you will lose your infection. **Do NOT continue to hunt tails.**

---

### Bearwere Forms

| Stat | **Bearwere** | **Feral Bearwere** | **Dire Bearwere** |
|------|---------------|--------------------|-------------------|
| **Str** | 45 | 80 | 120 |
| **Sta** | 45 | 80 | 120 |
| **HPs** | 0 | 300 | 350 |
| **Int** | -16 | -25 | -50 |
| **Notes** | — | — | Int resistable with Max AR |

The first form is a **quest reward** (lvl 30).  
The second form costs **50 cms (lvl 40)**.  
The third form costs **25 cms (lvl 50)**.

---

### Level 40 Spell Quest

> **Warning:** Many level 40 spell quest mobs are triggered, not timed spawns. If someone defeats them before you arrive, you may need to relog your quest with the NPC.

- Talk to your **class-specific NPC** in *Unkempt North (S)*. They will ask you to defeat a **Golden Gnoll** in *Crethley Manor* (SE of the undead house). It cons white, casts, and heals.  
- After defeating it, the NPC sends you to **Kierra** in *Mariel Village (C)* north of Wynd. She is a faerie at the bottom of a tree ramp.  
- Then, defeat a **Green Racer Snake** in *Unkempt Glade*. From Kierra, travel south to a lake surrounded by druids, then east to a hill where the snake spawns.  
- Alternatively, find the lake in *Unkempt South (C)*, run east to the road, follow it south to a quarry with 5 caves, then head north — the snake spawns on a hill by the road.  
- The NPC will send you to **Briggs** in *Salt Mines (C)*, located between the Slioth dock camp and the cave entrance.  
- Return to *Unkempt Glade* for your reward.

---

### Spells

**Cave Bear Strength** — *Rge, Brd, Ran, Mnk*  
> 98 STR buff that stacks with first bear form and shaman buffs.

**Growl of Battle** — *War, Sk, Pal*  
> 67 STR/STA group buff.

**Bear Nobility** — *Cl, Sha, Drd*  
> 122 CHA self-buff.

**Summon Blessed Fish** — *Mag, Nec, Wiz, Enc, Alc*  
> Summons a level 33 PoT/HoT drink item.

---

### Note: Doing Bearwere at 60

**Qnsprincess** says:

> Mirin conned deep red (with a light blue pet) to a level 60 — he's a caster mob found in *Unkempt North (S)* just west of Dorindas' hut (if you can't see the hut, you went too far). He also respawns instantly after triggering, so multiple players can do it in sequence. A level 60 alch and necro easily defeated him twice.

> My friend and I got infection in two days at level 60. We farmed **Murnf** for faction (1k with Wards of the Dawn) by killing weres near Grimm Brellhand (the 47 quest NPC). Many bearweres, wolfweres, and ratweres spawn there. Afterward, we farmed **SBM** for two hours post-server reset. After about 20 kills (white, dark blue, and light blue), we both got infected. Infection at 55+ seems much easier due to stronger mobs spawning more often.
Gatorwere:
**Quest Name:** Gator Were Quest
**Level:** 30
**Class:** All
**NPC:** Lis Boglurker
**Location:** Black Water
**Tail NPC:** The Bone Cruncher in Dinbak SE

Speak with **Lis Boglurker** in Black Water. She will tell you of her sorrows and the loss of her father. She will ask you to assist her in determining what fate has befallen him. To do so, travel west from BW into **Widow's Peak (E)**. There you will find three tents occupied by **Ivander Yaroslav**, **Isabella Yuroslav**, and **Dimitri Yaroslav**. Speak with them and return to Lis. She will ask you to again return to them. When you do so, they will despawn and reappear as KoS and the same level as the person who triggered them. One mob is a caster, the other two melee.

Defeat them and return to Lis. She will then send you to speak with **Yolanda Ribisi** in Oasis. Speak with her, then return to Lis, who will now send you to see the **Bone Crusher** in Dinbak (SE).

**Bone Crusher** sends you to seek out the two notorious gatorweres **Unga** and **Daraznak Vekiz** in Hazinak (NW). Look for two small tents just north of a camp of troll crusaders. There you will find the two mobs. After you have spoken with them, they will despawn and reappear KoS. One will con light blue to the person who triggered them, one white. Defeat them and return to **Yolanda** in Oasis, then back to the **Bone Crusher**.

Now you are given the tail part of the quest. Do so and start farming the appropriate level gatorwere until you receive the infection pop-up.

**Important Notes:**
- Don’t leave the infection part until later. Killing a yellow con gatorwere is critical to getting infected.
- Avoid having higher-level toons in your group, as they don’t reduce infection chance but can interfere with spawns.
- Check level vs. zone info to hunt yellow con gatorweres.
- You need at least 1000 faction to complete the infection process.

**Infection Locations by Level:**
**Level 30–40:** Dinbak (confirmed), Stone Watchers (lvl 35 confirmed), Lake Noregard (S), Brog Fens S/SE (Dustyspheres recommendation)
**Level 40–50:** Lake Noregard (E, W, C), Cazic Thule, Brog Fens (Blackwater), Mila's Reef, South Barren Coast (yellow con @47–49)
**Level 50–55:** South Barren Coast (LB @60, very difficult to get infected)
**Level 60:** The Hunt (lvl 60 confirmed)

After infection, **Bone Cruncher** sends you to defeat **Julian** in Dinbak (NW). Look for a raised mound surrounded by glowing mist. He will con red to the person who triggers him. Bring help. Return to **Bone Cruncher** after defeating him.

**Quest Rewards:**

| Form | Str | AC | HPs | Int |
|------|----|----|----|----|
| Gatorwere (lvl 30) | 45 | 80 | 0 | -16 |
| Feral Gatorwere (lvl 40) | 80 | 130 | 300 | -25 |
| Primordial Gatorwere (lvl 50) | 120 | 150 | 350 | -50 |

Int resistable with Max AR

**Level 40 Spell Quest Notes:**
- Many level 40 quest mobs are triggered, not on spawn timers. Scout before triggering to ensure you can complete the quest.
- Speak with class-specific NPC on Crocosaurus's island (Lake Noregard), then Fort Alliance (SE), then Taksehir city (Great Waste).
- Kill **Gypsy Treheart** (Burial Mounds S) and **Nullo** (Sycamore Joy's Rest N), both triggered mobs. Return to quest NPCs for reward.

**Spells:**
- **Growl of Battle:** War Sk Pal — 67 Str/Sta group buff at lvl 60
- **Reptillian Frenzy:** Rge Mnk Ran Brd — 120 Sta/Agi self-buff
- **Reptilian Gaze:** Cl Sha Drd — 107 Cha self-buff at lvl 60
- **Swamp Vines:** Nec Mage Wis Enc Alc — AoE root

**Tips:**
- Hunt low-level gatorweres west of the river south of Black Water.
- Gatorweres do not spawn on the east side of the river.
- West side open area spawns about 10–12 per hour.
Lionwere:
**Quest Name:** Lion Were Quest
**Level:** 30
**Class:** All
**Quest NPC:** Burgomaster Delvin
**Location:** Sondereel Village, Forkwatch (E), on river
**Tail NPC:** Corindas in Forkwatch (E), Sondereel Village.

**Walkthrough:**
Burgomaster Delvin asks you to escort **Lionell Redrick** safely through the Salt Mines. Travel to the Salt Mines (W) and locate Ridrick in the mine. Enter the mine and descend the spiraling stairwell — Redrick is in the first adjacent room. After speaking with him, he will begin to ascend the stairwell. When he reaches the top, you should receive a pop-up. Once Ridrick walks into the main entrance, three mobs will spawn and engage him (one necromancer, two melee). Focus on the necromancer first. After defeating them, Redrick continues on his way.

When he reaches the courtyard, he’ll be ambushed again by similar mobs. Defeat them, accompany Redrick to the tree, and have the quest holder speak with him to update the log. Return to Burgomaster for part two.

Travel north from GM into **Kelinar (SW)** and speak with **Herman Forrestor** in a small tent outside the city. After speaking, back up to avoid aggro. Three hunters spawn (dark blue), and Herman cons white. Defeat them, ensuring the quest holder is grouped when Herman dies. Return to Burgomaster for your reward.

Now log the **tail quest** with **Corindas**. If your faction (Villagers of Sonderal) is below 150, kill werelions in **Centaur Valley** to raise it. Ultimately, you’ll need **1000 faction with Sunmanes** to complete the infection quest.

**Infection Tips:**
- Kill **YELLOW**-conned weres for the best infection chance.
- If only farming faction, hunt lower-level zones.
- Having a high-level player in group **does not reduce** infection chance.

> “The rumor that having a level 60 in your low-level group affects your chance of infection just isn't true... I’ve helped nine infections with a level 60 bard doing all the killing.” — *Dustyspheres*

**Where to Get Infected:**

**Level 30–34**
- Kelinar
- Centaur Valley (hunt lionwere, not werelion)
- Forkwatch (S, SE – between the rivers near Sonderal Village)
- Widow’s Peak

**Level 35–39**
- Forkwatch: near the arches on the river at level 37. Kill wildlife to spawn yellow lionweres — one kill often infects you.
- Kelinar also works.

**Level 40–50**
- Fort Alliance
- Desert Hate
- Elephant Graveyard
- South Crossroads
- Wktaan’s 4th Talon
- Deathfist Horde
- South Barren Coast (white/yellow cons @ high 40s)
> Hunt where wildlife cons yellow for your level to maximize infection.

**Level 50–55**
- Abysmal Sea (51–60 range; yellow @51–55, LB @60)
- South Barren Coast (LB @60, low infection rate)
- Toxxulia South
- Sylhilthis’ Dwell
- Near Kappa Guards (elder elephant spawns, white @60)

**Level 60**
- Abysmal Sea
- Toxxulia South
- Sylhilthis’ Dwell
- Near Kappa Guards (white @60)

**Infection Confirmation:**
You’ll know you’re infected when Corindas gives you a popup saying he must urgently speak with you. You can also confirm in **Oasis**:
- **Advisor Gerahl** checks *any* were infection.
- **Advisor Sanne** checks *lion* infection.

After infection, Corindas sends you to defeat **Jensen** in **South Crossroads (N)**. If he’s not stationary, he may roam. Neutral before triggered, red after. Bring help. If he doesn’t spawn, re-speak to Corindas.

**If Corindas won’t progress:**
- Ensure **Sunmanes faction ≥ 1000**.
- Delete the tail quest, re-speak to him.
- **Never take the antidote** or you’ll lose infection.

**Lionwere Forms (Level 60 Stats):**

| Form | Sta | Agi | HPs | Int |
|------|-----|-----|-----|-----|
| Lionwere (30) | 45 | 45 | 0 | -16 |
| Feral Lion (40) | 80 | 80 | 300 | -25 |
| Dire Lion (50) | 120 | 120 | 350 | -50 |

Int resistable with Max AR.

**Level 40 Spell Quest:**
Triggered mobs — scout before activating.

**Steps:**
1. Speak with **Katerina Suncharmer** in Sondereel Village. She sends you to kill the **Undead Slave** in **Oasis** (first flag on left, down a small valley). Red to 40, white to 60.
2. Return to Katerina, then go to **Trizzia Newmane** in **Fort Alliance (SE)** courtyard. Say the correct line to receive the *Collar of Power*. Return to Katerina.
3. She then sends you to **Merry-By-Water (C)** to speak with **Branty** near the south entrance (across from the spiritmaster). Say the right thing to get the *Claw of the Ancients*. Return to Katerina.
4. Lastly, she sends you to kill **Zipnick** near **Zentar’s Keep**, behind the castle near the fisherman. Look for a stone circle with mist. Con red to trigger, white @60. Bring help and time your trigger carefully.

Return to Katerina for your reward.

**Spells:**
- **Lion’s Nobility:** Cl Sha Drd — +115 Cha self-buff @60
- **Accelerated Foliage:** Nec Mage Wis Enc Alc — AoE root
- **Growl of Battle:** War Sk Pal — +67 Str/Sta group buff @60
- **Agility of Kehrahn:** Rge Ran Brd — +80 Agi & SoW @50

**Were Tips:**
- In **Centaur Valley (SE)** — great for level 35s. Coach to Blackwater, head north, then NW across the river before the first guard tower.
- **Zipnick Spawn Tips:**
  - *(a)* If solo, let a mob kill you near spawn, log quest, then rez and kill Zipnick.
  - *(b)* If solo but can coach, turn in quest, coach to Zentar, wait 1–5 mins for spawn.
  - *(c)* If in group, have them wait in Zentar before you log the final quest part.

In **Abysmal Sea**, lionweres spawn in ~5 areas: two near the Hunt border, one near northern Kerran village with the boat, and others along the coast. Kill man-hunters and welahi-nai creatures to spawn them.

**Mob Info:**
- **Undead Slave** north of Oasis — red @40, LB @56.
- **Zipnick** likely level 50 (white @60).

**Notes:**
You can complete the second part even if you didn’t kill the hunters — just log the new quest from Herman and proceed.
Ratwere:
**Quest Name:** Rat Were Quest
**Level:** 30
**Class:** ALL
**Quest NPC:** Shady Swashbuckler
**Location:** Temby
**Tail NPC:** Yindrius in Freeport SE

Travel north along the shore from Freeport into Hodstock and Tembly. The Shady Swashbuckler is located in the fishing village. He will ask you to speak to the Shady Trafficker in the High Pass Castle.

Coach to HP and speak with the Trafficker. Two HP guards will spawn as will another mob which attacks the Dealer/Trafficker. Assure that the Dealer survives the battle, kill the two triggered HP guards and loot the NTL item from the one of them. THEN talk to the trafficker again... he'll despawn after the battle, then respawn at his original starting point. You must talk to the trafficker, he will take the coins that the swashbuckler gives. If you fail to do this, Rathei in FP won't talk to you and your quest will not advance. Return to the Swashbuckler.

The Swashbuckler will then send you FP to speak with Rathei Slairin. If you are familiar with the Spiritmaster Keika, Rathei is in the building just west of her. After you have done that you will be asked to speak with Yindrius. From Keika, travel east past the city wall, follow it north to the entrance and enter the city there. Yindrius can be found along the southern side of the building. Return to the Shady Swashbuckler.

After you have approached Rolby in MTG and spoken with Shady, he will ask you speak with Cogwhisker in Bandit Hills (N). From Darver Manor travel east into Bandit Hills. Cogwhisker is located in a bandit camp near the lake in the north section of the zone. You have two options: You can either pay him off with tunar or fight him, either is fine. **Rumour has it, if you kill him, you get 200 faction with Topi di Ombra.** If you do choose to fight him, back-up quickly to avoid argoing the mobs as they spawn. Cogwhisker will con white to the person who triggers him, his henchmen will con dark blue. Insure the person with the quest logged is in the kill-group and return to the Swashbuckler.

He will then send you to the Freeport to speak with Yindrius (see note below). Log the tail quest and farm the appropriate ratweres until you receive the pop-up.

**Note:** If you cannot log the tail quest with Yindrius, check your faction. Topi di Ombra faction must be minimum 150 to log the tail quest. Should your faction be less than this, kill ratweres to increase your faction.

You can get infected anywhere between 0 and 1700 faction, but cannot complete the infection process until your faction is equal to or exceeds 1000.

**Don't leave the infection part until later!** The sooner you start the easier it is to find were's that spawn yellow to you. Killing a yellow is critical to getting infected. Yes, it's possible to get your infection from light blues and reds, but the percentage chance of getting infected from a yellow is huge in comparison. So, if you're finding were's that spawn too small for you, try a different zone or a different part of the same zone.

**On having a level 60 in a group of lvl 30's:** There was always a rumour that having a higher level toon in your group to help with the killing would mean your chances of infection would drop almost to nil. Well here's some good information:

Dustyspheres tells us: *The rumor that having a level 60 in your low level group affects your chance of infection just isn't true in my experience. The last 9 infections I've gotten or helped get were all with the toons in one group, invised and on Auto-follow. These toons range from 33–39 and were on AF with a 60 bard that did all of the killing. All they did was loot tails and they still got infected. Some were in just a few kills and most were infected before anyone even had the required 1k faction to accept the infection.*

**Where to get infected:**

**Level 30–35** — Ferren's Hope (S/SE)

**Level 35–40** — Ferren's Hope (C)
*Note:* At level 37, in FH (C), yellow ratweres will spawn. For some reason, this is a great combination for infection. We have found it easier to be infected at level 37 in FH (C) than in any other part of the zone, or any other level.

**Level 40–50** — Tak 'Xiv West, Wktaan's 4th Talon
Dustyspheres: level 35–40 can get infected in Wktaan's 4th Talon (SW).

**Level 50–60** — Geomancer's Citadel (lvl 60 - confirmed), The Warrens (lvl 60 - confirmed), South Barren Coast

You will know you are infected when, on your approach to Yindrius to turn in tails, you receive a popup that says he must *urgently* speak to you. (See the note below for clarifications.)

**Note:** You can also check for infection in Oasis. Go to the campfire near the water, several NPCs stand around it. Advisor Gerahl checks for general infection (have you been infected by ANY were). Advisor Tihmpra checks specifically for rat infection.

After you have received the infection pop-up, assuming you have the correct faction etc., Yindrius will ask you to defeat Trissen in Bobble by Water. The mob will con red to the person who triggers him, bring assistance.

**Note:** If you get the popup that Yindrius has something urgent to tell you:
- If all he'll do is give you the tail quest again, check first: is your faction with Topi di Ombra 1000 or more? If not, get more faction by killing more weres.
- If your faction is good, delete the tail quest in your quest log, re-speak to Yindrius who will now give you the final part of your were infection quest.
- When Yindrius does give you his speech **Do NOT take the antidote**, or you lose your infection. **Do NOT continue to hunt tails.**

---

**Ratwere Forms (Level 60 Stats)**

| Form | Dex | Agi | AC | Notes |
|------|------|------|-----|--------|
| Ratwere | 45 | 45 | 0 | Int/Str resistable with Max AR |
| Feral Ratwere | 80 | 80 | 130 |  |
| Dire Ratwere | 120 | 120 | 150 |  |

---

**Level 40 Spell Quest**

*Warning:* It is believed many level 40 spell quest mobs are triggered, not timed spawns. If this is true, and someone defeats them before you arrive, you must relog the quest with the NPC to retry. Scout the area first.

Speak with the appropriate class NPC in Temby. They will ask you to speak with Nunnie in Urglunt's Wall (near the large cyclops house east of the main gate). Although she may be red-faced, she will not attack. Return to the NPC.

Next, speak with Trick Trune in Broken Skull Rock (NW), near the beach. Return to the NPC.

Then defeat a Tae Ew Trader in Cazic Thule (NW). The mob is triggered and sees through invis. Roam until you con it or are jumped.

Then go to Tak Xiz South to defeat the Tae Ew Hoarder. Cross the river into the west or center and search for a small hill surrounded by green mist. He triggers on approach, sees through invis, cons white, and is not soloable. Bring help.

Return to the NPC and collect your reward.

---

**Spells:**

- **Cunning Subterfuge** — Cl, Sha, Drd (90 CHA self-buff at level 59)
- **Swarm Mentality** — Wis, Enc, Nec, Mage, Alc (400ish group max-power buff)
- **Distracting Squecks** — Rge, Brd, Ran (AC debuff, 200–300)
- **Defence of the Swarm** — War, Sk, Pal (67 STR / 342 AC group buff at level 60)

*Saark, East Human Ratwere Defender of CoES, states this ability is modded by wisdom. At lvl 60, with 600 wisdom, you get 125 STR and 420 AC. Clerics AC Order (group-based) overrides this, but it stacks with Druid AC (target-based).*

---

**Were-tips:**

- Killing mobs spawns both Great Ratweres and Great Bearweres in The Warrens (E/NE). Level 60 necros can solo them (light blue). Respawn is slow, so wipe the valley and wait. You can get 4–5 Great Rats per night.

- Tried to get infected in Ferran's Hope S/SE (30–39) but failed. Tried Wicks and SBC, too high. Had max faction for 2 months, no infection. Then tried Ferran's Hope (C) — on the other side of the Blacktongue camp — and after about 6 rats, got infected!
Wolfwere:
**Quest Name:** Wolf Were Quest

**Level:** 30

**Class:** ALL

**Quest NPC:** Fryda Lightwolf in CLW

**Location:** Fryda is in the castle in North Wilderlands

**Tail NPC:** Levandius in the Unkempt Village, Unkempt North (S)

---

Speak with **Fryda Lightwolf** in Castle LightWolf. She will ask you to search for **Brother Norton**. To locate him, follow the road south from CLW. He is just past the Inn. Speaking with him triggers three mobs — one casts, the other two are melee. Their level is determined by the person who triggers them. After you have defeated them, return to Fryda.

Fryda will now send you to speak with **John Lightwolf** in the **Freezeblood Village (N)**. Run south from Halas into the wilderness and search the terrain for him. He can be found at the base of a small hill in the ice plains. Speaking with him triggers three mobs similar to the first part of the quest. Return to Fryda after you have defeated them.

Fryda will now ask you to speak with **Levandius** in the **Unkempt North (S)** to log the tail quest. He can be found in one of the suspended huts in the druid village. Log that quest and begin farming the appropriate wolfweres until you acquire an infection pop-up. If Levandius will not speak with you (i.e., you can't even log the tail quest), you'll need to hunt wolfweres first to raise your **faction with Night Reapers to 0 (≈150)**.

For infection, killing weres that **con YELLOW** to you is key. Yellow-con weres give you the greatest chance of infection. Check your level against the zone information below and ensure you are hunting yellow-con weres — otherwise, you'll only gain faction. If you are already infected and just need faction, it’s easier to hunt in lower-level zones.

---

### **Infection Rumor (Level 60 Help)**

There was a rumor that having a level 60 in your low-level group would reduce your infection chance. **This is false.**

> *Dustyspheres says:* “The rumor that having a level 60 in your low-level group affects your chance of infection just isn't true. The last 9 infections I helped with all happened while grouped with a 60 Bard doing all the killing. Lowbies (33–39) were invised, auto-following, looting tails — and still got infected quickly, most before even reaching 1k faction.”

---

### **Where to Get Infected**

**Level 30–40**
- Forkwatch
- North Wilderlands *(Lvl 34 confirmed)*
- Unkempt North
- Unkempt Glade

> *At level 37*, in Forkwatch near the **arches on the river**, is the perfect infection spot.
> Kill wildlife in that area to spawn **yellow wolfweres** — this is key. In multiple tests, killing a **yellow were at level 37 = instant infection.**

**Level 40–50**
- Salisearaneen (center-north, near dire worgs — TY Kemuell)
- Forkwatch (Lvl 40 confirmed)

**Level 50–60**
- Stoneburnt Mountains (Lvl 52–60 confirmed)
> *Note:* SBM is crawling with wolfweres!

---

### **The Hunt**

You’ll know you’re infected when, upon approaching **Levandius** to turn in tails, you get a popup stating he has something *urgent* to speak with you about.

> *You can also verify infection in Oasis.* Go to the campfire near the water — several NPCs stand around it.
> - **Advisor Gerahl:** Checks for *any* infection.
> - **Advisor Nitarha:** Checks *specifically* for wolf infection.

After infection, Levandius will ask you to defeat **Zaran** in **Unkempt North (C)**.

- Run west along the road from CLW, past the inn — a tower will be on your right.
- Zaran spawns just beyond it.
- His level scales with the person who triggers him — **bring help!**

---

**Note:** If you get the popup that Levandius has something urgent to tell you:

- If he only gives you the **tail quest again**, make sure your **Night Reapers faction** is at least **1000**.
- If your faction is fine, **delete the tail quest** from your log and re-speak to Levandius — he should now give you the **final infection quest**.
- When Levandius gives you his speech: **Do NOT take the antidote**, or you lose your infection.
- **Do NOT continue hunting tails.**

---

### **Form Stats (Level 60)**

| Form | Str | Sta | HPs | Int |
|------|-----|-----|-----|-----|
| Wolfwere | 45 | 45 | 250 | -16 |
| Feral Wolfwere | 80 | 80 | 300 | -25 |
| Dire Wolfwere | 120 | 120 | 350 | -50 |

**Int** is resistable with Max AR.

> *Note:* Stats scale — at level 30 you won’t get full bonuses yet, but you will as you level.

---

### **Level 40 Spell Quest**

⚠️ **Warning:** Many level 40 spell quest mobs are *triggered*, not timed spawns. If someone kills them before you arrive, you may need to **relog your quest** with the NPC quest giver to respawn it.

**Tips:**
- Scout the spawn area before triggering your mob.
- Or, have a guildmate camp the spawn while you log the quest to protect it.

---

### **Quest Steps**

1. Speak with **Edward Duskrender** (TY Pixie_FH) in the druid village, Unkempt North (S). He’s on the ground near the tree with Levandius.
2. Defeat **Martin Flicker** in **Mayfly Glade (E)** near the river.
   - Find two rocks; stand on the big one, face the forest.
   - Martin spawns near a fallen log ahead.
   - **Caster**, spawns **10 levels higher** than you.
   - Recommended: **High AR.**
3. Speak with **Dawn Raylight** in **Muriel Village (C)** and **John Dewey** in **Misty Thicket (NE)**.
4. Defeat **Jasmine**, a bear in **Surefall Glade (NE)**.
   - Found *within the city walls* — evil characters must invis/run from Honjour.
   - Jasmine **cons red** and is a **triggered melee-only** mob.
   - Have a guildmate camp the spawn area.
   - If someone kills Jasmine, **relog your quest** (don’t delete, just re-speak to Edward Duskrender).

Return to **Edward Duskrender** and **collect your reward.**

---

### **Spells**

**Growl of Battle**
> *War / Pal / SK*
- STR/STA group buff.
- Scales with level.
- At level 60 = +67 STR/STA.

**Rage of Drinal**
> *Ranger / Bard / Rogue*
- Self-buff: +75 base STR/STA.
- 100% INT mod on STR, 100% WIS mod on STA.
- Costs 185 power, lasts 2 min, stacks with forms.
- Debuff: -50% INT/WIS (resistable with AR).

**Howl of Terror**
> *Wizard / Nec / Enc / Mag / Alc*
- Sacrifices 220 of your mana to give group 240.
- Costs 240 power, 60s recast.

**Howl of Doom**
> *Cleric / Shaman / Druid*
- Group life tap.
- At level 40: 166 dmg, 83 HP drain.

---

### **Were-Tips**

- The first two quest-stage mobs don’t always scale perfectly — at level 60 they con white, at 52 they con red (~level 55).
- The final mob, **Zaren**, still spawns red to level 60 even when triggered by a 52.

**Jasmine’s Spawn Tip:**
> Jasmine spawns on the **NE end of the lake in Surefall**, near the shore in a triangle of trees (one with visible roots).
>
> Respawn is quick — ~5 minutes.
>
> Difficult but doable fight with help.
WereHunter:
**Quest Name:** Were-Hunter Quest

**Level:** All

**Quest NPC:** Djivan Mertshak

**Location:** Oasis

**PART 1 - Mandatory**

Speak with Djivan Mertshak in Oasis. He will give you a silver needle and ask you to search for a suspicious character with a pet rat in Hodstock.

In a small farming village in Hodstock (NW) you will find Slick Svenson and his pet rat, Bucktooth. When you poke Slick with the needle he and his pet turn into lycanthropes!

Their levels will be determined by the level of the person doing the quest. Both will con red and are not soloable. Be prepared for a fight. The spawn time on the mob is about 10 minutes.

Loot the NTL item and return to Djivan. He will then ask you to find a woman named Lynda Elmquist in Jethro's Pike.

Jethro's Pike is located in Jethro's Cast (S). There you will find Lynda standing in a small building. When you speak with her, she too turns into a lycanthrope! She, like Slick, cons red. Her respawn is about 5-15 minutes. Defeat the foul abomination and return to Djivan.

After you have spoken with Djivan he will ask you to speak with Marona Jofranka in Freeport.

Marona is located north of the coach near the dueling arena. She tells you to go speak with Dravin Slaerin in the Merchant House east of her. He is in the same building as Kradish Du'Klot from the Dead Hills quest.

Dravin gives you a pouch and tells you to go speak with Necrologist Ipstock in Hazinak.

Ipstock can be found standing by the river in Hazinak (C). When you speak with him, he too turns into a lycanthrope! He cons red and has a pet named "swarming minion." He, unlike the previous lycanthropes, does not drop a NTL. Assure that the person with the quest logged is in the kill-group when he is defeated in order to be "flagged." If accomplished, their quest-log should be updated. Return to Dravin.

Dravin then sends you back to Marona.

Marona warns you of a plot being conceived by an infamous vampire to summon undead beasts for what can only be assumed to be evil purposes. She tells you to return to the Oasis and alert Djivan.

In the Oasis, Dijvan gives you 2,000,000 XP. If you speak with him again, he asks if you want to join the Were Hunters.

**Note:** to complete this quest and be offered the werehunters brand you need a minimum of +500 faction with the Gypsies of Tunaria (not the Tunarian Gypsies). To get faction, head to Siloths and farm. The easier mobs at the top give +1 faction per kill, as get lower down, mobs give +5, while at the very bottom - each kill will give you +10 faction.

If you select "yes," he will put a "brand" on you preventing you from ever becoming infected by a lycanthropy. You will also receive a level 30 ring and now have access to the general were hunter CMS.

Congratulations, you are now an official Were Hunter!

**Rewards:**
Melee: Gypsy Honour Ring lvl30 str15 int15
Tank: Gypsy Honor Ring lvl30 str15 int15
Healer: Gypsy Honor Ring lvl30 str15 int15
Caster: Gypsy Honor Ring lvl30 str15 int15

Keep this ring. Eventually, as you progress your quest to becoming a Were Hunter, you'll be required to show the ring to have it upgraded. Tank one gets upgraded like this:
*Gypsy Honor Insignia Lvl55 str25 int25 necklace

**PART 2 - Optional**
By gokukicks, Yaeadane, edited by Singz (from Questmasters)

1. Get your Tunaria Gypsies faction to 500+ and speak again with Djivan Mertshak in Oasis.
2. Djivan sends you to intercept shipment on Siloth docks (located south of the cave, next to the river). On the docks, kill the blackscourge smugglers/siloth knights until you can loot a folded parchment and siloth coin purse.
3. Now go to the magically locked chest they were standing next to and open it. It basically says the folded parchment had some words you recite and it opens the box. Return to Djivan in Oasis.
4. Tell Djivan what the contents of the chest were and show him the coinpurse. He will then tell you to take the coinpurse to Marona Jofranka in Freeport.
5. Jofranka sends you to the Klick'Anon Necro guild to see Necrologist Quenka [located near where the books are for the Highbourne Storyteller quest]. Quenka will take the coin purse from you and release a free spirit. Talk to the spirit and he tells you of a vampire called Tserrina. Return to Oasis to talk to Djivan.
**Note:** the spirit is not around for long, talk fast; if it despawns before you've finished it won't hurt the quest.
6. Djivan will now send you to kill Tserrina who resides NW of the salt mine on a hill with a castle on it. You'll see battlebones fighting siloths (Salt Mines NW). Her coffin is on the 3rd floor. You're to kill her and return to Djivan. Ensure all questers are in the kill group as this is a kg flag.
**Note:** in order to open her coffin, you need an uncommon/rare drop called an "incantation" to open it; kill siloths on the various floors or kill a named arch-necro to get one.
Tserrina has some decent level-appropriate drops: Mistmoore Betrothal Gown (robe HP: 9300 DUR:65 Level:45) and Harnessed Soul Gem (HP: 9100 DUR:50 Level:40 INT:30 Power:125). Other drops include Staff, Mistmoore Betrothal Ring.
7. Djivan will reward you with 8.3million XP (un-booned) and Charm of Gemile (neck lvl 40 str15 wis30 healer item). Rewards will vary by class.

**Quest Name:** Were-Hunter Ability Quest
Level: 46+
Class: All
Quest NPC: Djivan Mertshak
Location: Oasis

**Note:** to log this quest you need a minimum of +500 faction with the Gypsies of Tunaria (not the Tunarian Gypsies). To get faction, head to Siloths and farm. The easier mobs at the top give +1 faction per kill, as get lower down, mobs give +5, while at the very bottom - each kill will give you +10 faction.

Talk to Djivan Mertshak in Oasis. Djivan tells you a long story about Mayong Mistmoore, a vampire who seeks control of the siloths. He asks for your help in defeating Mistmoore. Djivan tells you to see Enrique Revnold by the crossroads at Forkwatch.

Enrique talks about his ancestor, Vedel Revnold, a famous bard. Vedel crafted a song to rouse Mistmoore from his coffin, but Vedel was defeated and became the Maestro of Rancor. Enrique will give you a tome of Vedel's songs but in exchange wants a Writ of the Wild from the Unkempt Druids. Farm rangers and druids in the Unkempt Village (where Levandius is) [Unkempt North SW] to find your Writ of the Wild. Usually drops in about 15 minutes of farming.

Return to Enrique with the Writ of the Wild and receive Vedel's songs in exchange. He tells you the Maestro can only be summoned by playing the songs on an instrument crafted by a vampire.

You are to look for the instrument in the vampire's lair. Head to Saltmines (SW) - not the castle, the mines - find your way down the ramps several levels. Find Colen Ilbeth and enter the tunnel where he stands, go down some more ramps. Eventually, you find yourself entering a small room with 3 drachnids, clear them. Beyond that is a circular room with drachnids and a red spider graphic on the floor. Go to the second room on your right, clear the room, keep mobs down.

Approach the organ and get a popup, and Maestro of Rancor spawns in the middle of the carpet (CONs yel@lvl50). Kill the Maestro and loot a song page.
**Note:** if more than one questor needs to kill the Maestro, you will have to wait on the respawn time, which can be more than 30 min (i.e., this is not a group flag).

Now it's time to find Mayong Mistmoore. Head back out to the central room, and again head to the second tunnel on your right. You'll come across the room with the 'drachnid body snatcher'; clear this room. Pass through this room and down a tunnel, turn right at the brick-lined hallway, and STAY right. Follow the hall to the end, there will be a small room with two glyph familiars and a drachnid, clear the room and take the ramp down. In the large cavern down below you can clear mobs. You will be able to con 'stone coffin'; there are several down there. Con around and see if you con the coffin of Mayong Mistmoore. He is a rare spawn and you won't catch him here very often. If he is 'up', then assemble your team to take him down.
**Note:** you MUST be in the KS (kill group) group to advance your quest. Be in a group with 3 other DD's and have everyone else ungroup (this mob is easily one groupable though). DON'T DIE - if you die you have to restart the quest.
**2nd Note:** If more than one questor is at this exact spot in the quest (i.e., kill Mayong), as long as all questors are in the kill group, all will have their quest advanced.

Mistmoore has an AoE lifetap, so only the tank should face him head on; everyone else should hide from the AoE and pop out quickly to cast/melee etc. If he lifetaps you, it heals him and keeps the fight going longer. Kill Mayong Mistmoore, get the XP for the kill, and your quest log will advance.

Return to Djivan Mertshak in Oasis to get 1.4 million XP and a reward.

**Rewards:**
Melee: Troka's Blessing spell scroll + Talisman of Troka, necklace, NTL, Lvl50, Dex30, Int15
Tank: Blessing of Valentina spell scroll + Talisman of Valentina, necklace, NTL, Lvl50 str30 int15
Caster: Blessing of Yazmina spell scroll + Talisman of Yazmina, necklace, NTL, Lvl50 str15 int30
Healer: Blessing of Luludja spell scroll + Talisman of Luludja, necklace, NTL, Lvl50 str15 wis30

**Quest Name:** WereHunter Trials

**Level:** 50+

**Class:** All

**NPC:** Djivan Mertshak

**Location:** Oasis

Conflict between the Lycans and WereHunters grows Tense! Those who have accepted the werehunter brand may find new tasks in Oasis to undertake. Check out the other were hunter quest here!

**Quest Part I - Siloths**

See Djivan Mertshak in Oasis. When entering the village from the coach, his tent is on the far right with two assistants (south, s/w of coach).

**Note:** Djivan Mertshak will not speak with you unless you have a minimum of +500 faction with the Gypsies of Tunaria...see note below on how to improve your faction.

"Gypsies have recently learned that Lord Phinnius enlisted additional forces to keep watch over his enslaved workers. Perhaps Lord Phinnius only wishes for his wealth to grow but we cannot allow this atrocity to continue. Head to the saltmines and hinder the siloths in any way possible. It will provide a means to measure your devotion."

Translation: You need a minimum of +1851 faction with the Gypsies of Tunaria (not the Tunarian Gypsies). To get faction, head to siloths and farm. The easier mobs at the top give +1 faction per kill, as get lower down, mobs give +5, while at the very bottom - each kill will give you +10 faction. You can farm the faction in advance of logging this quest.

When you are ready, speak with his assistant Jhevin, who is standing next to him. If you make the minimum faction requirement he will give you an accomplishment token and tell you to speak with Djivan Mertshak again.

Do not reply to the NPC with rude comments or you are penalized by removal of faction.

**Quest Part II - Hunt + Gather for Faction**

Once you have obtained max faction, see Djivan Mertshak again in Oasis. Now begins the farming portion of this quest.

"Well done, you proved your devotion. The first step is to take this insignia as a token of dedication (Gypsy Honor Insignia Lvl 55, NTL, necklace, +25 str/dex)."

**Note:** you must be Lvl 55 for Djivan to give you the hunt and gather part of the quest.

He asks you to obtain stacks and sets (parts of werebeasts), when you turn in these stacks/sets your faction will increase (very slowly). You need to increase it to +2000.

A stack is 4 of the very same item from one were (like 4 lionwere tails). A set is 5 of the same type of item (like a tail from a lion, wolf, bear, rat and gator). Best to turn in items as sets instead of stacks since you will get more faction that way. Here are places to hunt for weres:

* **Lions**: Deathfist Horde East and Southeast, leonines spawn at night and last 1 hour. The EQOA sun sets at :55 and rises at :25. When the sun sets in this zone between the castle and the mountains, the 3 subzones will despawn all mobs and respawn leonines at night. You can farm them for the pristine items you will need as well as stacks you need for your faction quest. Keep killing the other wildlife as well and leonines will respawn at a good rate.
  *Tip:* Also, if you can get a druid or wizzy to port you to SBC and/or SBM for the other 30 min (when the leonines are down), the time flies by and the parts really add up.
  *Note:* During the day in Deathfist Horde, run to Wktann's 4th and grab any rats and wolfs that you can there; they will only provide tails but rat is the hardest to find so get what you can.
* **Gator**: Lake Noreguard, kill ancient gharails in lake, one gator will spawn per 4-5 kills, they drop pristine items needed also.
* **Rat, Lion, Gator**: head to south barren coast (especially after a server reset), there are 4-5 areas that spawn weres consistently when you farm the mobs there (I find wisps best placeholders). Rats with items needed other than tails can also be farmed in Geo Citadel.
* **Stonebrunt**: bear and wolf, usually can grab a full set of tails/items from them, and if you run in after farming SBC, the trip to cave entry will likely find you 1-3 rats and 5-6 bears.
* **SBC for rats, gators, and lions**: area around the southernmost ratman camp. Narrow area, easy to track spawns, large enough to constantly be killing. Note: zone crashes may spawn additional weres.
* **Saliseareen for bears and wolfs**: About 75% of the weres that spawn there are green, rest are LB. Easier to farm there lately since SBM has been packed.
* **Neonii comments**: best to farm sets on Thursdays after server reset. Using klic bow to kill wisps helps spawn more weres. Gators in Lake N. take more time but spawn better than in SBC.

**Inventory Note:** Have 25 inventory spaces available. It's advised to make a bank character in Oasis to transfer unused items while waiting to grab more.

**Reward:** The reward is a ring.
* Melee: Gypsy Were-Hunter Ring 13000/13000 Dur: 50 Dex: 45 CR: 5 Int: 20 FR: 5

BEFORE PROCEEDING TO THE TRIALS, YOU MUST COMPLETE THE ELDER TCHEKOF QUEST. Once complete, return to this quest and complete Part 3 - the Trials.

**Quest Part III - The Trials**

1. Talk to Delegate Tenrathe to start the WereHunter Trials. He is located in the SE part of Oasis near the fence close to the river. **Important:** Have your WereHunter ring along. He will send you to see Nivaan D'Tarin.
2. Nivaan D'Tarin is in Bogman (NE), on floating raft in the water. After some quest dialogue, Nivaan tells you to remember "But the warm air carries itself into the night." Nivaan will then port you to Wymondomen (N).

**1st Trial - Trial of Sight and Memory**

3. Once in Wymondhmen (N), speak to Trainer Iruko. He will go through some quest dialogue. Speak with him again to get the "Concentration Totem" and your TEN MINUTES will start. Trainer Iruko will port himself to a nearby hill. Go speak with him on this hill to be placed where HE wants you.

Once you are where he wants you, THREE different Were-Beasts will spawn in a line. Facing the line: left is MOB #1, right is MOB #3.

**Note:** Take a couple of minutes to identify which Were is where. Write them down quickly.

There will be three sets of three questions for the three sets of answers listed below.
* Set One Answers: 3-3-3
* Set Two Answers: 3-2-1
* Set Three Answers: 2-1-2

**Warning:** Incorrect answers or exceeding 10 minutes loses 75 faction with the WereHunters of Tunaria. Djivan Mertshak in Oasis now ONLY accepts sets (lion, wolf, rat, bear, gator). Tails-only sets are not accepted.

Set of hides/pelts = +6
Set of meats = +12
Set of claws = +16
Set of teeth = +18

Return to Wymondhem (N) to redo question portion if needed.

**Intermission**

5. Master Iruko tells you to seek out Irlynn Poe near a bridge in Jared's Blight zone. Poe is located under the bridge on the east side of the river near Darvar Manor coach. Poe will upgrade your WH ring: Before: Arcane WH Band, After: Radiant Arcane Band (+5 FR/CR, +5 LR/AR).

**2nd Trial - Trial of Scent and Tracking**

6. Go to Cyclops's Fortress (C) on a hill with a "T"-stone to find Master Landaar. He will guide you on scent tracking and mark a captured/released were-beast. Gather allies to assist; the captured were-beast is stronger than prior mobs. A 15-minute clock starts when he instructs you to begin.

**Popups:**
* Popup #1: West of Master Landaar, North side of second rock.
* Popup #2: South/Southwest from #1, middle of triangle formed by 3 trees.
* Popup #3: East/Southeast from #2, next to a big rock near northernmost Cyclops tent.
* Popup #4: North from there, North crest of hill near T-stone and Master Landaar.

**Warning:** Do NOT go directly to the were-beast; approach widely.

The were-beast spawns as "A Vicious xxxWere" and has a lot of HP. Kill it and loot drops. If failed, speak to Master Landaar to redo the 1st Trial (no faction lost unless answering wrong).

7. After killing the were-beast, speak with Assistant Urtoe (near kill location). He congratulates you on completing 2nd Trial and welcomes you to the WereHunter Council. Visit Delegate Rhinni in Oasis.

8. Delegate Rhinni upgrades your ring and gives you your archetypical ability.

**Tank Ability:** Hunters Scorn - Invisible shield around you, maddening to enemies. Scope: Self. Cost: 500 pow. Range: 20. Cast: 2. Recast: 3. Lvl 55. 50 DS/Hate shield lasts 10 minutes.

**Healer Ability:** Combat Healing - Heals comrade at close range. Scope: Target. Cast: 3. Recast: 6. Power: 500. Range: 15. Lvl 55.

**Melee Reward:** Precious Strike - Scope: Target. Cast: 1. Recast: 30. Power: 300. Level 55. Range: 5.0.

**Caster Reward:** Shards of the Hunter - Magical shards pierce almost any armor. Level 55. Cast 2. Recast 3. Power 300. Range 10'. Luminous Arcane Band: Level 60 NTL, HP 13000, Dur 70, Str 20, Int 45, +5 to All Resists.

**MC Requirements:** Each MC costs 200 cms. Minimum stats required per archetype. Includes Arcane Rejuvenation, Arcane Regeneration, Heroic Wit 1-3. CM database by TeamJosh for updates.

**Gypsy Were-Hunter (Melee):** 50 max agi, 50 int, 10 HoT, 1 Off mod, 2 def mods.
**MCA Gypsy Traditions:** 25 sta, 25 agi, 25 dex, 25 int, 25 fr, 25 ar, 10 mov.
**MCA Stake:** Wooden stake delivers devastating blow to vital organs.

**Heroic Were-Hunter (Tanks):** 50 Str Max, 50 Int, 1 def mod, 2 off mods, 10 HoT.
**MCA Vital Strike:** 75 CM's. Melee strike (str/int moded), recast 5 sec.
**MCA Danger Awareness:** Agi+25, FR+25, Wis+25, Int+25, LR+25, 1 Defensive mod.

**Divine Were-Hunter (Healers):** 50 Str, 50 Wis Max, 10 PoT, 10 HoT, 2 Def Mods.
**MCA Unyielding Dedication:** 25 STA, 25 DEX, 25 WIS, 250 HP MAX, 1 OFF MOD.
**MCA Burning Blow:** Cast 2, Recast 5, Power 427, Range 30. Two numbers: str mod and wis mod.

**Arcane Were-Hunter (Casters):** Int-Max 50, Str 50, Sta 10, Dex 10, Agi 10, Wis 10, Cha 10, PoT 20.
**MCA Arcane Endurance:** Sta 25, Agi 25, PoT 5, CR 25, HoT 10, Dex 25, FR 25.
**MCA Molten Silver of Solist:** 1250 direct dmg, 500 dot ticks x4, AR based, 30s recast, 600 pow.

**Quest Name:** Elder Tchekof

**Level:** 60

**Class:** All/All

**NPC:** Elder Tchekof

**Location:** Oasis

Begin this quest when you have 2k+ faction via Were-Hunter Trials.

Talk to Elder Tchekof (~50 feet NW of oasis coach). Bring Wererat disguise from Dram Corma and 1 tail from each were (lion, bear, rat, wolf, gator).

Dram Corma: repeatable side quest, sends you to Deathfist Horde (E) to find Yrin Croto for wererat disguise and letter to HP/FP messenger.

Messenger: HP - Polinee Hirbo, requires 4 platinum sprockets from Cape Dreg clockworks. Rewards: 2 million XP

Return to Elder, give wererat disguise and tails. He gives weregator disguise.

**Combat Note:** Were mobs may deathtouch. Have someone aggro with pet first, tank engages afterwards.

**Mobs:**
* The Bone Cruncher: Dinbak (SE). After death, take tooth to Elder.
* Yindrius: FP, tough. Give fur to Elder.
* Corindas: Sondereel Village, give claw to Elder.
* Dorandas: Unkempt North (S), give tail to Elder.
* Levandius: wolfwere, NE snow/grass, 5 guardians (yellow), kill Levandius (red), give eye to Elder.

Elder sends you to Delegate south of coach. Completing this grants access to new WereHunter CMS.
Vampire:
### **Quest Name:** Vampirism
**Level:** 30–60  
**Class:** All  
**NPC:** Greddy Oldman  
**Location:** Freeport

---

Vampirism Vampir10Vampirism Vampir11

**Nov 21/08:** Apparently the bats are back up and Dr. Killian in FP will remove any abilities from *corrupted* lycans/WH that got initially infected by accident. Bats spawn much more slowly than they did in the past, appearing for around 10 minutes in any of the numerous zones they have been spotted in. Some good news though — mortalkombat says that a desmodus bat that **CONs green to you can still infect you.**

First, in order to be turned into a Vampire, you cannot be either a werehunter or a werebeast; you must have a *clean* toon.

---

### **The Angry Patron**
Found wandering the halls of Freeport, will tell you some lore:

> Don't bother me! I'm not in the mood!  
> *Ask him what happened?*  
> Some folks ain't got no sense! This guy at the Inn is a bit too big for his britches if you ask me. Going on about how great he is, and how his boss could bring down the Gods themselves! I told him to shut his trap, but he just laughed. And of course my buddies, who I thought would back me up... just laughed with him. I ain't one to run from a fight, but I ain't standing for humiliation either.

(This popup is not required, but just fills in some background.)

---

### **1. Logging the Quest**

Speak with **Greddy Oldman** in Freeport, located roaming in the warrior's guild (on the right-hand side as you're running to the bank from the coach).

> **Note:** If Greddy will not talk to you, this has nothing to do with faction, and is probably the result of a latent lycan infection. You could be infected without even knowing it, and having never done the quests. Greddy has a great nose and can sniff a lycan a mile away. Head to Oasis and speak with the 5 NPCs around the fire near the river; if you are infected, have it removed and then speak again with Greddy in FP.

Greddy will tell you that **Razeal has won**, and that the *filthy creatures* have been released into Tunaria to spread their disease. He says they are everywhere and never stay in one place too long, and that you are to be careful — you never know which one of them is going to infect you! He gives you a crumpled note (illegible):

> S--n in B-r---l M----ds, Je----'- C-s-, Gu---ian -or--t, -id--'ts P---, -om- -f -in-s, a-d Co--on--d-e C-m-t-r

(Again, this part of the quest appears not to be required to get infection.)

> **Note:** Once the quest is up and running again, Oldman will check your status as a lycan or WH and if clear, flag you to hunt for the bats. It is only those that have THIS flag that will be able to get infected going forward (this is to eliminate cross-contamination of werebeasts/werehunters and lycan).

If you are already a werebeast with x-contamination, you're supposed to be able to speak with **Dr. Killian** in FP to have your corrupted abilities removed.

---

### **2. Infection**

Farm the various camps of **Desmodus Bats** (different con colors @60) that have sprung up in six different locations around Tunaria. They are supposed to despawn during the daylight hours of EQOA. Infection is a **group flag** (even if 30s are grouped with 60s). You do not need to be hit to be infected, and greens will infect.

Bats will be rotating their spawn locations so that all zones will see bats at least once per day, but you won't know where in the zone they will spawn. As each of the 6 stated zones will have 3–4 different camps, you must look through the whole zone to see if bats are up. If a camp despawns, look around — odds are it respawned somewhere else in that zone.

---

### **Guardian Forest Locations**

- **NorthWest (east of northern gob camp):** Bats spawn between two snowy rocks just NE of the camp.  
- **NorthEast:** Found outside the Freezblood Berzerker Camp.  
- **SouthWest:** Near the southern gob camp; there’s a cave ~20 yards SW in the grass.  
- **Center (C):** In the grassy area near a tree north of white-faced mobs — cave with bats around it.  
- **SW of Elders:** Large green hill with blue light visible from the hill.  
- **Draethen (SW):** East/southeast of treant camp.  
- **Dustyspheres:** East of NE Treant camp, down in the valley.

---

### **Collingridge Cemetery**

- **East:** Cave on the west side at the bottom of two hills near the Crest of the Duelists.  
- **West:** Just up the road from the cemetery entrance.  
- **North:** Along the river, near a tower with two Dragoons (Y'Ziken & X'Talin).

---

### **Widow's Peak**

- **East:** First hill NW from the gatorwere NPC camp.  
- **NorthEast:** Right by the big rock next to the gnoll camp (cave despawns).  
- **South:** Near mountain in the middle of four towering stones.

---

### **Burial Mounds**

- **NorthEast:** Near Taksehir craftsman (for Broyce Sandstalker quest).  
- **SouthWest:** Near Greymoon Cemetery.

---

### **Jethro's Cast**

- **East:** 3–4 camps total. One bat cave on NE side of a mountain, another SW of that.

---

### **Tomb of Kings**

- **NorthEast:** From the valley entrance, head straight north to grassy hill with fog-covered T-stone and moss-covered tree.  
- **East:** Somewhere outside.

---

> Reminder: A Desmodus Bat that **CONs green** can still infect you.

Once you see a bat cave with bats in any of the six zones, it will respawn again in that very spot in **27 hours.** Infection is random; you might get it on the first cave or the third.

If infected, you may notice a DoT (-50) during daylight, but to check for sure, speak with **Grindal** in Freeport (Inn on left from coach). If infected, he will acknowledge it — if not, farm more bats.

---

### **3. Level 30 – Indoctrination**

Grindal ports you to **Sanctuary** (hidden room in ZP) — large underground area with NPCs and coffins.

Speak with **Hinder (lvl 30)** who explains you have been blessed and will gain the ability to feed. Acquire full stacks of **Blood Units** as all vampire abilities require them as reagents.

You also gain:

**Feed**  
*Primary method for gaining Units of Blood (used to power all Vampire abilities)*  
- Level: 30  
- Cast: 3  
- Recast: 3  
- Scope: Target  
- Blood stacks: 30 (No Trade / No Rent)

**Sanctuary** *(cost: 2 Units of Blood)*  
Teleports you to Sanctuary and allows porting between connected coffin locations. Usable once per day.

**Coffin Port Locations:**  
Freeport (SE)  
Guardian Forest (C)  
Vastly Deep (C)  
Solusek’s Eye (SE)  
Sslathis (S)  
Spider Mines (E)  
Collinridge Cemetery (C)  
West Feerrott (C)  
Zaoran Plateau (NW)

**Debuff:** During daylight hours, a -50 DoT effect applies. Prevents invis/sow pots.

---

### **4. Level 40 – Further Training**

Speak with **Creed** in Sanctuary. Grants **Vampiric Strike** and **Vampire Form.**

**Vampiric Strike (Lifetap)** *(cost: 7 Units of Blood)*  
- Level: 40  
- Cast: 1  
- Recast: 12  
- Scope: Target  
- Damage: ~1200–1500  
- Heal: ~400

**Vampire Form** *(cost: 2 Units of Blood)*  
- Level: 40  
- Cast: 3  
- Recast: 15  
- Scope: Form  
Visual form only (no stat buffs).

---

### **5. Level 50 – Weaver’s Lessons**

Speak with **Weaver** in Sanctuary. Grants **Bat Form** and **Vitality.**

**Bat Form (Offensive)** *(cost: 8 Units of Blood)*  
- Level: 50  
- Cast: 3  
- Recast: 30  
- Duration: 30s  
- Strength set to 500, AC -963, Mana 1/1. Offensive buff at cost of defense.

**Vitality** *(cost: 5 Units of Blood)*  
- Level: 50  
- Cast: 1  
- Recast: 60  
- Duration: 60s  
- Healing + defensive buff (+1000 HP, +300 AC)

---

### **6. Level 60 – Final Mastery**

Speak with **Simmons** in Sanctuary. Grants **Frenzy**, **Mist Form**, and **Vampiric Gaze.**

**Frenzy** *(cost: 5 Units of Blood)*  
- Level: 60  
- Cast: 1  
- Recast: 60  
- Duration: 60s  
Offensive buff that increases damage per hit by 500 but reduces defense.

**Mist Form** *(cost: 8 Units of Blood)*  
- Level: 60  
- Cast: 1  
- Recast: 30  
- Duration: 30s  
Changes you to Red Mist, defensive buff (+2000 AC, boosts defensive mods, sets STR=0, Power=1/1)

**Vampiric Gaze** *(cost: 10 Units of Blood)*  
- Level: 60  
- Cast: —  
- Scope: Target  
Sets aggro with target to 0.

Simmons also hints that 12 hidden relics exist in 3 groups of 4 — their nature unknown since the scholar who translated the scroll vanished. Her names include **Tharith Neda**, **Jaena Armec**, and **Sienn Talbat.**
